LAW AND MOTION 302, **COURT DISCLOSURE: IN 1997 AND FOR A FEW YEARS THEREAFTER THE COURT WAS CO-COUNSEL ON A PATENT CASE WITH STOEL RIVES. THERE WAS ONE- PERHAPS TWO- SUCH CASES; THEY INVOLVED THE SAME CLIENT. THE COURT DOES NOT RECOGNIZE ANY OF THE NAMES OF THE STOEL RIVES ATTORNEYS IN THE MOTION PRESENTED TO THE COURT. THE ATTORNEYS THE COURT WORKED WITH HAD THEIR OFFICES IN PORTLAND, OREGON. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE STOEL RIVES HAD A SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE AT THAT TIME. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE, AND THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT A PERSON AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD HAVE ANY REASON TO BELIEVE THE COURT COULD NOT BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL.*** RULING: DEFENDANTS STOEL RIVES, LLP, EDWARD DUCKERS, STEVEN LOVETT & STEVEN KLEIN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON MOTION TO STAY THIS ACTION- INCONVENIENT FORUM. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, ***COURT DISCLOSURE: IN 1997 AND FOR A FEW YEARS THEREAFTER THE COURT WAS CO-COUNSEL ON A PATENT CASE WITH STOEL RIVES. THERE WAS ONE- PERHAPS TWO- SUCH CASES; THEY INVOLVED THE SAME CLIENT. THE COURT DOES NOT RECOGNIZE ANY OF THE NAMES OF THE STOEL RIVES ATTORNEYS IN THE MOTION PRESENTED TO THE COURT. THE ATTORNEYS THE COURT WORKED WITH HAD THEIR OFFICES IN PORTLAND, OREGON. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE STOEL RIVES HAD A SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE AT THAT TIME. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE, AND THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT A PERSON AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD HAVE ANY REASON TO BELIEVE THE COURT COULD NOT BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL.*** RULING: DEFENDANTS STOEL RIVES, LLP, EDWARD DUCKERS, STEVEN LOVETT & STEVEN KLEIN'S MOTION TO STAY THIS ACTION - INCONVENIENT FORUM - IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: WILLIAM GWIRE, ESQ., 415.296.8880, FOR PLAINTIFFS AFD CHINA INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY OFFICE ET AL.
JOSEPH MCMONIGLE, ESQ., 415.438.4534, AND JOHN SULLIVAN, ESQ., 415.397.2222, FOR DEFENDANTS STOEL RIVES LLP ET AL.

COURT DISCLOSURE: IN 1997 AND FOR A FEW YEARS THEREAFTER THE COURT WAS CO-COUNSEL ON A PATENT CASE WITH STOEL RIVES. THERE WAS ONE- PERHAPS TWO- SUCH CASES; THEY INVOLVED THE SAME CLIENT. THE COURT DOES NOT RECOGNIZE ANY OF THE NAMES OF THE STOEL RIVES ATTORNEYS IN THE MOTION PRESENTED TO THE COURT. THE ATTORNEYS THE COURT WORKED WITH HAD THEIR OFFICES IN PORTLAND, OREGON. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE STOEL RIVES HAD A SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE AT THAT TIME. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE, AND THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT A PERSON AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD HAVE ANY REASON TO BELIEVE THE COURT COULD NOT BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL.

RULING: MATTER ARGUED. THE COURT TAKES DEFENDANTS STOEL RIVES, LLP, EDWARD DUCKERS, STEVEN LOVETT & STEVEN KLEIN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON MOTION TO STAY THIS ACTION- INCONVENIENT FORUM. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
